behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the contract between a real embedding model output and index dimension. A stubbed embedder can be fixed to the expected size and hide real failures (model/version/config drift). Not Workflow because behavior text targets a single embedding output property, not multi-step agent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model used in production for a fixed query, then assert returned vector length equals the live index dimension (from index metadata/config) before insert/search.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: This is module handoff correctness between search service and vector DB/query layer (ordering semantics). It does not require real OS/network/subprocess, and does not depend on AI output quality if vectors/corpus are controlled. Unit is too low because ordering can break at DB adapter/query boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local test vector index with known vectors, run similarity search through service+repository wiring, and assert returned scores are monotonically non-increasing and IDs match expected rank.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM judgment for nuanced intent; deterministic mocks hide whether steering/prompting actually changes ranking. Not Workflow because behavior text is specifically one re-ranking decision over a provided candidate list.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a fixed query plus fixed top-K candidates where lexical similarity conflicts with contextual intent, invoke the real re-ranker model, and assert output order differs from raw similarity order in at least one position.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is threshold/empty-state propagation across API->search->filter modules. A controlled local index can reproduce it without real OS/network/subprocess. Agentic was considered, but behavior text disqualifies it by focusing on output policy ('returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise') rather than model semantic quality.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local index with vectors intentionally far from a test query (or force low similarity scores), execute request through API and search/filter stack, and assert response is an empty list when all scores are below relevance threshold.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: This behavior measures full pipeline latency across multiple agentic steps in sequence (embedding call + LLM re-rank), plus service and vector search. Mocks can hide real network/model latency distributions. Higher than System because the requirement is explicitly end-to-end over chained agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like end-to-end load test through the real REST endpoint with real embedding and re-ranking model calls, collect per-request latency over a representative sample, and assert p50 < 2.0s.
